name: standup-formatter
description: Use when generating standup reports, daily updates, or team status summaries. Formats work activity into concise standup format.
capabilities: Formats work activity into concise standup reports
Provides templates for individual and team standups
Focuses on outcomes rather than activities
Supports both async written and sync spoken formats
author: ChaiWithJai
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation-based skill with no executable code. All 19 static findings are false positives: the scanner misidentified markdown code formatting (backticks) as shell execution, and misinterpreted text descriptions containing words like 'report' and 'update' as cryptographic patterns. The skill only contains guidelines and templates for formatting standup reports.
valueStatement: Struggling to write concise daily standups that actually help your team? This skill transforms your work activity into properly formatted standup reports that focus on outcomes, not just activities.
limitations: Does not automatically fetch data from external sources
Requires manual input of completed work and plans
No integration with project management tools
Cannot track or store historical standup data

useCases.title: Daily Development Updates
useCases.description: Transform git commits and code reviews into concise standup reports that highlight actual progress and blockers

useCases.title: Team Status Aggregation
useCases.description: Compile individual updates into a unified team standup that identifies cross-dependencies and shared blockers

useCases.title: Async Team Communication
useCases.description: Create detailed written standups for distributed teams across different time zones

promptTemplates.title: Basic Daily Standup
promptTemplates.prompt: Format this into a proper standup: Yesterday I fixed the login bug and merged PR #123. Today I'm working on the payment integration.

promptTemplates.title: Team Standup Compilation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a team standup from these individual updates: Alice finished user testing, Bob fixed 3 bugs, Carol waiting on design approval

promptTemplates.title: Weekly Summary Format
promptTemplates.scenario: Convert weekly work to standup format
promptTemplates.prompt: Transform my weekly activities into a standup format focusing on key deliverables: Completed user authentication and database migration

promptTemplates.title: Blocker-Focused Standup
promptTemplates.prompt: Format this update to emphasize blockers: Implemented API endpoints but tests failing due to database issues

bestPractices: Focus on completed outcomes, not hours worked or activities performed
Keep each section to 2-3 bullet points maximum for clarity
Make blockers specific and actionable with clear help needed
antiPatterns: Listing every single task or meeting attended
Using vague descriptions like worked on stuff or made progress
Saying no blockers when actually stuck or waiting on something

faq.question: Is this compatible with my team's existing standup format?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates follow industry-standard standup formats and can be customized to match your team's specific style.

faq.question: What if I have more than 3 items to share?
faq.answer: Focus on the most important 2-3 items. Save additional details for follow-up discussions or async channels.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my project management tools?
faq.answer: Currently this is a standalone formatting tool. You will need to manually input your work items from other tools.

faq.question: How does this handle sensitive project information?
faq.answer: The skill processes text locally without sending data to external services, keeping your project details secure.

faq.question: What if I am stuck but do not know how to phrase the blocker?
faq.answer: The skill helps identify and phrase blockers clearly, suggesting specific help needed rather than vague statements.

faq.question: How is this different from using a generic text formatter?
faq.answer: This skill understands standup best practices and automatically structures content for maximum team communication value.
